resync.title: Sync data from PowerSchool
resync.description: Pull the latest staff / parent / student records from the school system.

schedule.title: Automatic sync schedule
schedule.body: In production the system syncs from PowerSchool every 24 hours · press 'Sync Now' below to trigger an immediate sync (e.g. after adding a new staff member).

resync.loadingDescription: Pulling data from PowerSchool — please wait.

pricing.title: Internal vs Retail pricing
pricing.body: Choose Internal for staff/family/internal use (cost price) — choose Retail for regular customers. The system charges accordingly.

negLimit.title: Negative Credit Limit
negLimit.body: This student can purchase with a negative wallet balance, up to the set limit. The system warns when the limit is exceeded.

returns.title: Product Returns

flow.title: Return / Exchange Flow
flow.body: 1) Search receipt · 2) Pick items to return (watch the remaining returnable qty) · 3) Pick exchange items (if any) · 4) Choose refund destination: Wallet (refund to student wallet) or Cash (pay out) · 5) Provide a reason and confirm.

returns.popupBlocked: Cannot open print window — please allow pop-ups

receipts.title: Receipts

statusGuide.title: Receipt Status
statusGuide.body: Active = normal receipt · Voided = receipt cancelled (cannot be undone) · Search by receipt number, payer name, payment method, amount, or date · For QR/cash receipts without a payer, search by receipt number or amount

receipts.searchReceipt: Search by receipt ID, name, amount, or date...

void.title: Void Transaction

void.searchPlaceholder: Search by receipt ID, product name or barcode...

irreversible.title: Voiding a receipt cannot be undone
irreversible.body: Pressing 'Void' permanently marks the receipt as voided, returns stock, and refunds the wallet (if paid via wallet) · A reason is always required for audit trail.

reports.deptUseSeparated: Department Use is tracked separately from normal sales.
reports.deptUseExcludedFromGrand: Grand Total in this report covers normal sales only — Department Use is excluded.
reports.title: Reports

exportFormat.title: Export Format
exportFormat.body: Pick a date range (both start and end days are included) → the system exports CSV with BOM encoding so Thai characters open cleanly in Excel · Works with Excel / Numbers / Google Sheets.

reports.salesReportDesc: Daily, monthly and yearly sales summary
reports.stockReportDesc: Current stock and low stock items
reports.selectDateRangeDesc: Please select date range for the report
reports.stockCardReportDesc: Per-SKU movement history with opening and closing balance
reports.salesSummaryReportDesc: Per-receipt summary with payment-method breakdown, filterable by date, customer, family, or receipt range
reports.salesByItemReportDesc: Per-item sales activity grouped by SKU with quantity and amount totals
reports.salesByPaymentReportDesc: Sales broken down by payment method (cash, wallet, EDC, QR, department) with receipt counts and totals

stockWorkflow.title: Stock Management Workflow
stockWorkflow.body: Add products with the Add Product button · Adjust stock item-by-item with the ↕ icon (in the table) · Import in bulk via the Import button · View movement history in the Movements tab

importCsv.title: CSV Format
importCsv.body: Required columns: product_code, name, external_price · Optional: barcode, category, internal_price, vat_percent, avg_cost, stock, min_stock — upload a file or paste CSV below

staffRoles.title: Staff Roles
staffRoles.body: Manager = manages menu/products/staff · Cashier = uses POS to take payments only · Admin manages everything — each shop must have at least one manager.

settings.title: System Settings
settings.subtitle: Runtime feature flags for the whole system
settings.allowUserNeg: Allow User wallet to go negative
settings.allowUserNegHint: Staff and parent personal wallets can go below 0 when this is ON.
settings.allowCustomerNeg: Allow Customer wallet to go negative
settings.allowCustomerNegHint: Student/visitor wallets can go below 0 when this is ON. When OFF, admin can still grant per-customer overdraft via 'negative credit limit'.
settings.deptNote: Department wallets always allow negative balance — this policy does not apply to them. Admin manual adjustments also bypass these flags (they have their own audit trail).
settings.coverImageHint: Displayed on the left side of the Login page — landscape 16:9 or wider recommended

families.description: Link parent accounts with student accounts (family view)

families.confirmDeleteDesc: This will unlink the selected parent from the child account.

scope.title: Family-level management

walletAdjust.title: Wallet Adjustment

auditReason.title: Reason is required
auditReason.body: Every adjustment is recorded in the audit log together with the admin's name and reason — use a clear reason so it can be traced later (e.g. 'Fix wrong balance from incident #123').

cards.title: Card Management (UID Mapping)
cards.description: All cards bound to staff / parents / students / visitors — synced from PowerSchool

intro.title: What is UID mapping?
intro.body: UID is the RFID card number read at the POS · PowerSchool sync maps UID → user automatically · To edit/unbind, click the view icon and edit from the owner's profile page.

limits.title: Daily Limit vs Negative Credit
limits.body: Daily limit = cap on daily spending · Negative credit = allow purchases with a negative wallet balance (e.g. guardian hasn't topped up yet) up to the set amount.

shopImport.title: Import data (.xlsx / .csv)
shopImport.noErrors: No errors detected — safe to import.
shopImport.previewNote: This is a preview — no data has been saved yet.
shopImport.twoSheetsTitle: Why does the template have 2 sheets?
shopImport.explainerProducts: Products — product master data (name, price, category, UoM). Idempotent — re-importing updates names/prices without affecting stock.
shopImport.explainerStock: StockReceive — logs each receiving event (quantity + cost per unit). Not idempotent — re-importing the same file will double-count stock. Use only when goods physically arrive.
shopImport.explainerNote: One template covers both — fill in whichever sheet applies and pick the matching import option from the dropdown.

canteenMgmt.title: Canteen Management
canteenMgmt.description: Manage all canteen stalls — menus, categories, and settings

guide.title: User Guide
guide.subtitle: Step-by-step workflows for each role
guide.footer: For additional help, contact your system administrator.

roles.adminDesc: Manage users, wallets, cards, and system-wide settings
roles.parentDesc: Monitor children's wallets, top up, and view transaction history
roles.canteenDesc: Accept orders, process payments, and close daily sales
roles.storeDesc: Sell products, handle returns, and manage inventory
roles.managerDesc: View sales overview, manage products, staff, and reports

step1.title: Manage Users
step1.desc: Go to Admin Ops → Users. Create or sync staff, parent, and student accounts. Assign roles and shops. Use the Directory tab to search and assign existing PowerSchool accounts.

step2.title: Manage Cards
step2.desc: Go to Admin Ops → Card Management. View all RFID card bindings. Each card is linked to one user (student, staff, or parent). Click the view icon to go to the owner's profile and rebind if needed.

step3.title: Adjust Wallet Balances
step3.desc: Go to Admin Ops → Wallet Adjustment. Search for a student by name or code. Select Credit (add) or Debit (deduct), enter the amount and a clear reason. Every adjustment is recorded in the audit log.

step4.title: Transfer Between Wallets
step4.desc: Go to Admin Ops → Wallet Transfer. Look up a parent or staff account, select the source and destination wallet, enter the amount, and confirm. Use this to move funds between a parent and their children.

step5.title: View Reports
step5.desc: Go to Canteen Reports or Store Reports. Select a date range and click Export Excel. Reports cover sales, stock, returns, payments, top-selling items, and profit & loss.

step6.title: System Settings
step6.desc: Go to Admin Ops → Settings. Toggle wallet negative balance policy for users and customers. Enable per-student overdraft limits via the student profile page.

step7.title: Add Students & Members
step7.desc: Go to Admin Ops → Cardholders → + Add Cardholder. Choose Kind: Student (fill name, student code, grade), Staff/Parent (fill username + password so they can log in), or Other. The wallet is created automatically. To bind an RFID card, open the cardholder profile and edit the Card UID field.

step8.title: Set Up Department Accounts
step8.desc: Go to Admin Ops → Cardholders → + Add Cardholder → Kind: Department. Enter a Department Code (e.g. D0001) and Department Name. A department wallet is created automatically. To top up or debit, go to Admin Ops → Dept Wallet Adjust, select the department, choose Credit (+) or Debit (−), enter the amount and reason. You can add reusable reason chips (orange + Add button) that all admins share. Pick a date range in the history card then click PDF/Excel to export. To allow a shop to accept department charges, edit the shop and enable Allow Department Charge.

step9.title: Hybrid Accounts (Multi-role)
step9.desc: A single account can act as both Manager and Parent (or any combination). Go to Users, open the account, scroll to the "Additional roles" card, pick a role from the dropdown, then click + Add role. At login the user will see a Role Picker. No duplicate accounts needed. The family_code field is editable for every role (parents, staff, students) in the Edit form — use the same code to group a parent with their children.

step10.title: Bulk Import Products / Stock from Excel
step10.desc: Go to Shop Management → pick a shop → tab Inventory. Click Download template to get one .xlsx with two sheets (Products + StockReceive) with sample rows. Fill the file, then click Import data → pick Products or Stock receipt → choose the file. The system runs a Preview first showing would-create / would-update / would-import counts plus the first 50 error rows. Review, then click Confirm import to actually save. Managers can only import into their own shop; admins can import into any shop.

step11.title: Inspect the Audit Log
step11.desc: Go to Admin Ops → Audit Logs to see every system action, newest first. Each row spells out who-did-what-when in plain language (e.g. "Sold 1 item for ฿40.00 via Wallet — Thai Iced Milk Tea ×1"). Click View changes to see the field/value table translated into readable labels. Filter by Entity type, Action, or date range, then Apply filters.

step1.title: View Children's Wallets
step1.desc: Go to My Family from the sidebar. You will see all linked children with their current wallet balance, card status, and allergy info.

step2.title: Top Up a Wallet
step2.desc: Click Top Up next to any child's name. PromptPay shows a QR code to scan from your banking app. Credit/Debit redirects to the secure bank-hosted payment page (Krungsri EASYPay) — you enter your card on the bank's site, not inside ISB. Maximum ฿50,000 per transaction; credit card fee is 3%.

step3.title: View Transaction History
step3.desc: Click History next to a child's name. Filter by date range to see all purchases, top-ups, refunds, and adjustments. Click any transaction to view the full receipt.

step4.title: Manage Card Settings
step4.desc: Click Profile for a child. You can freeze or unfreeze their RFID card and set a daily spending limit (leave blank for unlimited). Changes take effect immediately at the POS.

step1.title: Accept an Order
step1.desc: Go to Canteen POS. Tap menu items to add them to the cart. For items with options (e.g. spice level), the customisation dialog will open — complete required options before adding.

step2.title: Process Payment — Wallet
step2.desc: Tap Charge → select Wallet → the 'Tap Card or Enter Code' dialog opens. Scan an RFID card OR type: Student Code (e.g. S12345), Card UID, Staff Username, or Department Code (e.g. D0001). Press Search — the system auto-detects who it is. Verify the name and remaining balance, then press Confirm.

step3.title: View Receipts & Void
step3.desc: Go to Canteen Receipts to see today's sales. Filter by date, payment method, or receipt ID. Click a receipt to view full details — shop name, cashier, payer account, and the balance remaining at the time of purchase. Cashiers can void receipts for their own canteen stall directly from this page — click the void (ban) icon, select a reason, and confirm. The wallet balance is refunded automatically.

step4.title: Daily Sales Summary
step4.desc: Go to Canteen Reports (manager access required). Select today's date range and export to Excel. The report includes per-item sales, total revenue, and payment method breakdown.

step5.title: Pre-select a Member Before Ordering
step5.desc: Click the 🔍 Search Member button (top right of POS). Type a name, student code, staff username, or department code — results include students, staff, parents, and departments. Select the payer first, then add items to the cart. When ready, press Charge — the system charges their wallet directly without re-asking for the card.

step6.title: Department Charge
step6.desc: Two ways to charge a department: (1) Wallet flow — enter the department code (e.g. D0001) in the RFID modal and press Search. (2) Department flow — tap Charge → Change payment method → Department, then select the department from the dropdown. Note: the shop must have Allow Department Charge enabled by an admin.

step1.title: Sell Products
step1.desc: Go to Store POS. Scan a barcode or search by product name/code to add items to the cart. Select a price mode (Retail or Internal). Choose the payment method and complete checkout.

step2.title: Process Payment
step2.desc: Payment methods: Member Card (RFID tap), QR PromptPay, Cash, or Department charge (employee code or card required). For cash, enter the amount received and the system calculates change.

step3.title: Handle Returns
step3.desc: Go to Store → Returns. Search for the original receipt ID (cashiers see only their own shop's receipts). Select the items to return and specify quantities. Choose refund method (wallet or cash) and enter a reason. Confirm to process.

step4.title: Check Inventory
step4.desc: Go to Shop Management → Inventory. View current stock levels, low-stock alerts, and movement history. Use Receive Stock to log new incoming goods and adjust costs.

step5.title: View Receipts & Void
step5.desc: Go to Store Receipts to see all sales. Filter by shop, date, or payment method. Cashiers can void receipts for their own shop directly from this page — click the void (ban) icon, select a reason, and confirm. Voiding is irreversible; stock is restored and wallet balance is refunded automatically.

step1.title: View Sales Overview
step1.desc: Go to the Admin Dashboard (admin) or your shop's Reports page. See today's canteen and store sales, low-stock alerts, and the last 10 transactions in real time.

step2.title: Manage Products
step2.desc: Go to Canteen Menu or Shop Management → Inventory. Add, edit, or remove products. Set retail and internal prices. Adjust stock quantities and receive new shipments via Receive Stock.

step3.title: Manage Staff
step3.desc: Go to Users. Add cashiers to your shop via the Directory tab (search existing accounts) or Create New tab (manual accounts). Assign or reassign staff between shops. Unassign removes POS access without deleting the account.

step4.title: Export Reports
step4.desc: Go to your module's Reports page. Select a date range, choose a report type (sales, stock, returns, payment methods, top-selling, or profit & loss), and click Export Excel.

step5.title: Configure Shop Settings
step5.desc: Go to Shop Management → your shop. Update shop name, description, and status (active/inactive). The inventory type (FIFO or Average Cost) cannot be changed after creation.

step6.title: Bulk Import Products / Stock from Excel
step6.desc: Go to Shop Management → your shop → tab Inventory. Click Download template for a sample .xlsx with two sheets. Fill it in, then Import data → pick Products or Stock receipt → choose the file. The system runs a Preview showing would-create / would-update counts plus errors. Click Confirm import to save. You can only import into your own shop.
